source.description: Built-in image generation created a six-panel marker process contact sheet for the cassette-tape-sticker lesson.
source.notes: The contact sheet was reviewed before cropping; the final panel is used as the finished asset.

process_strategy: The six frames keep one front-facing cassette sticker, introducing the rounded body, reels, label window, decorative stripes, bold outline, marker fills, and small highlights before the final pass.

final_elements.name: rounded cassette body
final_elements.description: A rounded rectangle forms the cassette sticker silhouette.

final_elements.name: center label window
final_elements.description: A simple central label window connects the reel area without text.

final_elements.name: two tape reels
final_elements.description: Two circular reels sit evenly across the cassette body.

final_elements.name: bottom stripe and screw dots
final_elements.description: A bottom stripe and tiny screw dots add cassette detail.

final_elements.name: thick black outline
final_elements.description: A bold black marker outline wraps the cassette, reels, window, and stripe.

final_elements.name: bright marker fills
final_elements.description: Teal, pink, yellow, and gray marker fills make the cassette feel like a sticker.

frames.visible_job: Draw the rounded cassette rectangle with light marker lines.
Add the blank label window near the top of the cassette body.
Draw the two even tape reels and simple connector shapes below the label window.
Add the bottom stripe and small screw dots without adding any text.
Thicken the black outline and add teal, pink, yellow, and gray marker fills.

final_step.visible_job: Retrace existing cassette, reel, window, and stripe edges, even out the marker fills, and add tiny highlights only to existing shapes.

rejection_checks: No major contour, prop, marking, or color choice should first appear in the final image.
No frame may darken, ink, color, shade, or clean a feature that was not already visible in an earlier frame.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
